sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management
sources.population: Adults with suspected or established chronic heart failure.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, community, and specialist cardiovascular care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult chronic-heart-failure diagnosis, review, monitoring, and clinician-led management; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.4–1.1.9 — primary-care review, record, care plan, and access
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.12 — history, examination, natriuretic peptides, imaging, ECG, and laboratory tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.4–1.7.11 — medicine-related monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led medication review and monitoring of renal function, electrolytes, blood pressure, pulse, and ECG without autonomous prescribing or dose output.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — clinical monitoring and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of functional capacity, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medication, renal function, haemoglobin or iron status, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Venous thromboembolic diseases: diagnosis, management and thrombophilia testing
sources.version: NICE guideline NG158, last updated 2 August 2023
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: People presenting with signs or symptoms of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism and people receiving related follow-up.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial medical assessment and clinician-led diagnostic pathways for suspected venous thromboembolic disease.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting a swollen or painful leg history, physical examination, conditional DVT or PE risk features, and clinician-entered assessment context. It does not calculate Wells or PERC scores, infer DVT or PE, order tests, prescribe anticoagulation, or determine urgency.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.2 and Table 1 — DVT history, examination, and clinician-scored features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting swollen or painful leg context, general medical history, physical examination, laterality and swelling or tenderness findings, immobility, surgery, malignancy, previous DVT, and a clinician-entered score only. No score is calculated.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.15–1.1.17 and Table 2 — PE history, examination, and clinician-scored features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting chest pain, shortness of breath, haemoptysis, vital-sign and examination context, prior VTE, immobilisation or surgery, malignancy, and a clinician-entered score only. No diagnosis or score is inferred.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.2.4 — clinician-agreed monitoring and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting an already agreed monitoring or follow-up plan and information already provided for confirmed or suspected low-risk PE; it does not generate that plan.
